revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:神名帳考証]]
{{Citation method|date=August 28, 2017 (Mon) 07:19 (UTC)}} {{Special characters}} {{Otheruses|Deguchi Enkyō's 『'''Jinmyōchō Kōshō''' (神名帳考証)』|Ban Shintomo's work of the same name|Jinmyōchō Kōshō Dodai}} 『'''Jinmyōchō Kōshō''' (神名帳考証)』 (jinmyōchō kōshō) is a work that examines the shrines (shikinaisha) recorded in 『{{ill|Engi-shiki Divine Name Register|de|Engi-shiki Götternamenverzeichnis|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engi-shiki Divine Name Register|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}』. It is authored by {{ill|Deguchi Enkyō|de|Deguchi Enkyō|ja|出口延経|lt=Deguchi (Doka) Enkyō|lt_ja=出口（度会）延経|lt_de=Deguchi (Doka) Enkyō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Deguchi Enkyō|1=Deguchi Nobutsune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q108780926}}, and it comprises 8 volumes. Enkyō, lamenting that many shikinaisha had unknown divine names and installation sites, undertook their examination and documentation. In the {{ill|Kanbun (era)|en|Kanbun (era)|qid=Q1146228|lt=Kanbun period|1=Kanbun (era)}} (mid to late 17th century), his work was succeeded by the two‐volume 『'''Jinmyōchō Bōchū''' (神名帳傍注)』, which was researched and examined by, among others, {{ill|Ise Shrine|en|Ise Shrine|qid=Q687168|lt=Ise Grand Shrine|1=Ise Grand Shrine}}, {{ill|Grand Shrine Priest|de|Großschreinpriester|ja|大宮司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Grand Shrine Priest|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宮司|1=Gūji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11453500}}, {{ill|Ōnakano no Sadanaga|de|Ōnakano no Sadanaga|ja|大中臣精長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnakano no Sadanaga|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 河辺精長|1=Kiyonaga Kawabe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q92924151}}, and by the father of the Outer Shrine official, {{ill|Deguchi Enka|de|Deguchi Enka|ja|出口延佳|lt=Enka|lt_ja=延佳|lt_de=Deguchi Enka|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Deguchi Enka|1=Deguchi Nobuyoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q62601522}}, as well as {{ill|Iwaide Suekiyo|de|Iwaide Suekiyo|ja|岩出末清|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwaide Suekiyo|1=Iwade Suekiyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q18700675}}, {{ill|Nakanishi Nobuyoshi|de|Nakanishi Nobuyoshi|ja|中西信慶|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakanishi Nobuyoshi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, among others. He worked for 30 long years until his death at the age of 80 in {{ill|Shōtoku (era)|en|Shōtoku (era)|qid=Q1193848|lt=Shōtoku|1=Shōtoku (era)}} 4 ({{ill|1714|en|1714|qid=Q6878|lt=1714|1=1714}}), but never saw its completion. Later, the Outer Shrine official Kuwahara Hiroo, along with the Hirose father-and-son pair, took the posthumous manuscripts left by Enkyō’s {{ill|Adopted son|de|Adoptivsohn|ja|猶子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Adopted son|1=yūshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11572068}}, namely those of {{ill|Arakida Takeaki|de|Arakida Takeaki|ja|荒木田武明|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakida Takeaki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and—after correcting errors and removing duplications—prepared a clean copy; it was completed in {{ill|Kyōhō|en|Kyōhō|qid=Q1145048|lt=Kyōhō era|1=Kyōhō}} 18 ({{ill|1733|en|1733|qid=Q6972|lt=1733|1=1733}}) with an introduction by Hiroo and a colophon by Hirose ({{ill|Afterword|en|Afterword|qid=Q677297|lt=Colophon|1=Afterword}}). According to Hirose’s colophon, the draft was initiated on July 8 of Kyōhō 17 and finalized on March 27 of Kyōhō 18. The structure of the 8 volumes is as follows: Volume 1 covers {{ill|Imperial Court|de|Kaiserhof|ja|宮中|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Court|1=Kyūchū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11453221}}, {{ill|Stadtmitte|en|Stadtmitte|qid=Q243779|lt=Capital area|1=Stadtmitte}}, and {{ill|Yamashiro Province|en|Yamashiro Province|qid=Q749276|lt=Yamashiro Province|1=Yamashiro Province}}. Volume 2 covers the four Kinai provinces: {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|qid=Q907495|lt=Yamato|1=Yamato Province}}, {{ill|Kawachi Province|en|Kawachi Province|qid=Q1047150|lt=Kawachi|1=Kawachi Province}}, {{ill|Izumi Province|en|Izumi Province|qid=Q1047117|lt=Izumi|1=Izumi Province}}, and {{ill|Settsu Province|en|Settsu Province|qid=Q864163|lt=Settsu|1=Settsu Province}}, comprising the Kinai ({{ill|Kinai|en|Kinai|qid=Q906708|lt=Kinai|1=Kinai}}) region. Volume 3 covers 4 provinces of the Tōkaidō: {{ill|Iga Province|en|Iga Province|qid=Q1047128|lt=Iga|1=Iga Province}}, {{ill|Ise Province|en|Ise Province|qid=Q864166|lt=Ise|1=Ise Province}}, {{ill|Shima Province|en|Shima Province|qid=Q1053722|lt=Shima|1=Shima Province}}, and {{ill|Owari Province|en|Owari Province|qid=Q514506|lt=Owari|1=Owari Province}}, along the Tōkaidō ({{ill|Tōkaidō (road)|en|Tōkaidō (road)|qid=Q963514|lt=Tōkaidō|1=Tōkaidō (road)}}). Volume 4 covers 11 provinces of the Tōkaidō, from {{ill|Mikawa Province|en|Mikawa Province|qid=Q768158|lt=Mikawa|1=Mikawa Province}} to {{ill|Hitachi Province|en|Hitachi Province|qid=Q1055353|lt=Hitachi|1=Hitachi Province}}. Volume 5 covers 8 provinces of the {{ill|Tōsandō|en|Tōsandō|qid=Q1334011|lt=Tōzan-dō|1=Tōsandō}}. Volume 6 covers 7 provinces of the {{ill|Hokurikudō|en|Hokurikudō|qid=Q3139239|lt=Hokuriku-dō|1=Hokurikudō}}. Volume 7 covers 8 provinces of the {{ill|San'indō|en|San'indō|qid=Q1323423|lt=San'in-dō|1=San'indō}}. Volume 8 covers 23 provinces of the routes of {{ill|San'yōdō|en|San'yōdō|qid=Q1376722|lt=Sanyō|1=San'yōdō}}, {{ill|Nankaidō|en|Nankaidō|qid=Q1376747|lt=Nankai|1=Nankaidō}}, and {{ill|Saikaidō|en|Saikaidō|qid=Q1334003|lt=Saikai|1=Saikaidō}}, as well as the two islands {{ill|Iki|en|Iki|qid=Q296720|lt=Iki|1=Iki}} and {{ill|Tsushima|en|Tsushima|qid=Q52048|lt=Tsushima|1=Tsushima}}. Furthermore, as a successor to this work, there exists a version in which {{ill|Matsuki Tomohiko|de|Matsuki Tomohiko|ja|松木智彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuki Tomohiko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} excerpted the Ise section from the original, titled 『'''Ise-koku Jinmyōchō Kōshō''' (伊勢国神名帳考証)』—of which there are two types, one from Shōtoku 3 ({{ill|1713|en|1713|qid=Q6873|lt=1713|1=1713}}) and another from Kyōhō 7 ({{ill|1722|en|1722|qid=Q6918|lt=1722|1=1722}}). In addition, to that "Ise‐koku Jinmyōchō Kōshō", {{ill|Hashimura Masanobu|de|Hashimura Masanobu|ja|橋村正身|lt=Hashimura Masanobu (はしむらまさのぶ)|lt_ja=橋村正身（はしむらまさのぶ）|lt_de=Hashimura Masanobu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hashimura Masanobu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} later added his reexamination, which he orally transmitted to his disciples, and which was recorded in writing in {{ill|Meiwa|en|Meiwa|qid=Q1146161|lt=Meiwa era|1=Meiwa}} 6 ({{ill|1769|en|1769|qid=Q7679|lt=1769|1=1769}}) as 『'''Jinmyōchō Kōshō Saikō''' (神名帳考証再考)』. There is also 『'''Jinmyōchō Kōshō Kenroku''' (神名帳考証撿録)』, to which {{ill|Deguchi Enken|de|Deguchi Enken|ja|度会延賢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Deguchi Enken|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} contributed additions, and Ban Shintomo’s work, 『{{ill|Jinmyōchō Kōshō Dodai|de|Jinmyōchō Kōshō Dodai|ja|神名帳考証土代|lt=Jinmyōchō Kōshō|lt_ja=神名帳考証|lt_de=Jinmyōchō Kōshō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinmyōchō Kōshō Dodai|qq=}}』, completed in {{ill|Bunka|en|Bunka|qid=Q1373146|lt=Bunka|1=Bunka}} 10 ({{ill|1813|en|1813|qid=Q6937|lt=1813|1=1813}}). ==References== * {{ill|Toki Masakun|de|Toki Masakun|ja|土岐昌訓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toki Masakun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} "Jinmyōchō Kōshō" (『Shintō Historical Dictionary』, {{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|de|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|1=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q4346755}}, Heisei 16, ISBN 4-642-01340-7) * {{ill|Yamaji Kōzō|de|Yamaji Kōzō|ja|山路興造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaji Kōzō|1=Kōzō Yamaji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11470970}} "Jinmyōchō Kōshō: Explanation" (『Collection of Materials on Japanese Commoners' Life History』, Volume 26, Shrine Origins, {{ill|San-Ishobō|de|San-Ishobō|ja|三一書房|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/San-Ishobō|1=San-ichi Publishing Co., Ltd.|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10865224}}, Shōwa 58) * "Shintō Great Dictionary" (reprint edition), {{ill|Rinkawa Shoten|de|Rinkawa Shoten|ja|臨川書店|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rinkawa Shoten|1=Rinsen Shoten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11612454}}, Shōwa 44, ISBN 4-653-01347-0 (first edition by {{ill|Heibonsha|en|Heibonsha|qid=Q11482108|lt=Heibonsha|1=Heibonsha}}, Shōwa 14) ==Related topics== * {{ill|Jinmyōchō Kōshō Dodai|de|Jinmyōchō Kōshō Dodai|ja|神名帳考証土代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinmyōchō Kōshō Dodai|qq=}} ==External links== * 『[https://kotenseki.nijl.ac.jp/biblio/100132933/viewer/1 Jinmyōchō Kōshō]』 - New Japan Classical Texts Comprehensive Database {{Shintō Side}} {{List of Shikinaisha}} {{translated page|ja|神名帳考証|version=87791773|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[ja:神名帳考証]] [[de:Jinmyōchō Kōshō]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{wikidata link|Q11588824}} [[Category:Shinto literature]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]
